intent: A container process (container_t with an MCS category pair s0:c123,c456) denied read+getattr on a host bind-mounted file labeled default_t (the host file was not relabeled to container_file_t for the mount). Container-host case on el10. Maps to one clean narrow allow; audit2allow -R substitutes files_read_default_files().
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds} (libsepol/audit2why replay; audit2why reports 'should be dontaudit'd' which is the TeAllowable wording on a dontaudit'd access, identical to scenario 1). floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected}. IMPORTANT floor note: scontext carries MCS categories (s0:c123,c456) while tcontext is plain s0. This is NOT an MLS/MCS mismatch the floor should flag - an MCS subject with categories DOMINATES a category-free object at the same sensitivity (s0), so the access is MCS-permitted and the denial is purely a TE gap. A floor heuristic that naively string-compares the level fields and flags MlsSuspected would be WRONG here.

oracle.narrower_than_R_by: -R substitutes files_read_default_files() (read_file_perms padding on ALL default_t files + default_t dir list) for container_t. Our expected.te emits exactly `allow container_t default_t:file { read getattr };` - the 2 denied perms only, no macro, no padding, no dir grant.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: Two traps. (1) A naive floor that compares the scontext level (s0:c123,c456) to the tcontext level (s0) and declares MlsSuspected would WRONGLY decline - an MCS subject with categories dominates a category-free object at the same sensitivity, so this is a plain TE denial, not a constraint. (2) Mirroring -R would emit files_read_default_files(container_t), granting the container read of all default_t files. The narrow fix is the 2 denied perms; the real remedy is relabeling the bind-mounted host path to container_file_t with the :z/:Z mount flag.
